name: Poland

colors.symbolism: from the white eagle of the Polish coat of arms; also peace and purity
from the red field of the coat of arms; valour and the blood shed for the nation

description: Two equal horizontal stripes — white over red.
history: Adopted on 1 August 1919 by the newly independent Second Polish Republic. The white-and-red colour combination derives from the medieval coat of arms of the Kingdom of Poland (a white eagle on a red field), and was used as a national cockade during the November Uprising of 1831. The flag was confirmed in its present form by the 1980 Coat of Arms Act, with the precise shades fixed in 1995.

features.symbolism: the heraldic colours of the Polish coat of arms — a white (silver) eagle on a red field — flipped per the heraldic convention that places the metal above the tincture
